response.justification: The candidate is a solid senior full-stack engineer with ~15 years of experience, strong software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development), and some genuine high-scale exposure (low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic). However, the role is a Principal/IC6 position centered on experimentation and growth systems, and the resume shows no experience with experimentation platforms, feature flagging, A/B testing, telemetry/metrics infrastructure, or growth engineering. There is also little evidence of principal-level scope: no multi-team architectural leadership, cross-org initiatives, or platform ownership serving multiple teams. The profile reads as a strong senior/staff generalist rather than a principal experimentation-systems engineer.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and backend systems with required languages (JavaScript/TypeScript, Java, C#, C/C++)
Real high-scale distributed systems experience: low-latency, async I/O microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong engineering fundamentals and mentorship signal: TDD, testing tooling authorship, workshops, code katas, and recent AI-agent/MCP tooling work
concerns: No experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, or growth-measurement platform experience — the core domain of the role
No demonstrated principal-level scope: no evidence of leading multi-workstream initiatives or building systems adopted across multiple teams
Limited telemetry/metrics infrastructure and statistical analysis experience relative to the role's data-aptitude requirements

key_factors.factor: Strong engineering fundamentals and scale experience
